title: Mohit Minerals Pvt. Ltd. v. Union of India
legal_principles: GST Council recommendations are not binding law
relevant_acts: Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act 2017
summary: Struck down the government notification levying IGST on ocean freight for imported goods on reverse charge basis. The Court held this amounted to double taxation (freight included in CIF value already taxed through Customs). Also held that GST Council recommendations are recommendatory and not binding — Parliament and state legislatures retain legislative power.
key_holdings: IGST on ocean freight under reverse charge mechanism on CIF imports is unconstitutional (double taxation)
GST Council recommendations are recommendatory and not binding on Parliament or state legislatures
Constitutional scheme preserves sovereignty of Parliament and state legislatures
Notification imposing IGST on ocean freight struck down
key_quotes: The GST Council is a recommendatory body and its recommendations do not have the force of law.
Double taxation of the same economic activity is impermissible.
why_important: Landmark GST case clarifying power of GST Council and striking down double taxation on ocean freight. Highly relevant to importers.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Directly relevant to GST compliance, import tax planning, and understanding limits of GST Council authority.
